rationale.completeness: All three requested features are implemented: PDFParser for 10-K/10-Q PDFs, FinancialCalculator with CAGR/ROE/ROIC/WACC and more, and FredAPI for macroeconomic data, plus .env.example and requirements.txt updates.
rationale.correctness: Several edge case bugs exist: CAGR validation uses `not all([start_value, end_value, years])` which fails when any value is 0 (falsy); similar issue in ROE, EPS, and other calculations where 0 is a valid input but treated as missing; PDFParser rejects non-.pdf URLs that could still serve PDF content; FredAPI makes a separate HTTP call for series metadata on every request which is wasteful and can fail silently.
rationale.style: The code follows the existing Tool base class pattern correctly and integrates cleanly into get_agent.py alongside existing tools; minor style issues include inconsistent use of `import io` inside try blocks rather than at the top of the file, and the Tool ABC is redefined in finance_tools.py rather than imported from a shared location.
rationale.quality: The file is 888 lines in a single module with no separation of concerns between three distinct tool types; the Tool ABC is duplicated from what likely exists elsewhere; error handling is inconsistent (some methods use try/except, others rely on validation checks); the silent fallback from pdfplumber to PyPDF2 with no logging makes debugging harder.
